description: Skipping repair beyond gc_grace_seconds lets deleted data resurrect across replicas.
body: Failure mode: anti-entropy repair is not run within gc_grace_seconds, so a replica that missed a delete's tombstone keeps the old value; once the tombstone is garbage-collected elsewhere, that stale value propagates and the deleted row reappears. Detection signals: previously deleted rows return in reads, repair logs show gaps or failures, and the repair interval exceeds gc_grace_seconds. Do not let the repair cycle lapse past gc_grace_seconds. Safer fallback: schedule incremental or subrange repair (or a reaper) to cover every table within the gc_grace window, alert on missed cycles, and run a full repair before trusting deletes after an outage.
